note: Run registry for the CPU lexical baselines (these do not go through run_manifest.py). Failed runs are retained, never overwritten, and their output directories are not reused.

runs.purpose: First full-target-set lexical baseline (name_match + bm25 x 3 folds x 3 views).
runs.log: logs/lexical_baselines_full.log (subsequently truncated by the replacement run; the exit line LEXFULL_EXIT=143 and partial results were read before truncation)

termination.cause: Operator-issued `pkill -f run_lexical_baseline` while relaunching to pick up the exact/tolerant CSR hash change. The kill and the relaunch were issued in one compound command, so the replacement raced the kill and never started; the job was left dead with the log frozen at 07:38 UTC.
termination.pid: not captured (the launcher did not record the PID; only the process name was matched). Fixed for later runs by logging the PID at launch.

runs.progress_at_termination: completed through fold1_seed42/bm25/missing; fold2_seed79 not run

outputs.reason: the script serializes results only after all combinations finish

runs.provenance_gap: started before the sparse_matrix_exact_hash / sparse_matrix_tolerant_hash change, so its partial numbers lack publication-grade CSR provenance and are not citable.

runs.purpose: Clean restart: first citable lexical result (ledger R-042).
includes: full target set per fold (no sampling)
exact + tolerant CSR hashes with recorded rounding precision
vocabulary/tokenization, candidate-order, view and targets hashes
runs.output_directory: DBP5L/ind_v2/audits/lexical/LEX-RUN-002/ (fresh; LEX-RUN-001's path is not reused)
